channel.title: Asclepius — changelog
channel.description: Cryptographically chained release history for the Asclepius clinical-AI trust substrate. Each entry references the previous hash; the changelog is itself a Merkle log.

item.title: v0.1.0 — The substrate, open-sourced.
item.description: First public release. C++20 runtime with signed Merkle ledger, composable policy chain, drift detection, consent registry, and offline-verifiable evidence bundles. CLI, Python bindings, three worked examples. 52 doctest cases passing. Apache 2.0.

item.title: v0.0.9 — Conformance: L1 audit suite green.
item.description: Pinned the on-disk ledger format (spec.md v1) and ran the L1 conformance suite end-to-end against three reference workloads. Sign-input layout fixed. Drift severity bands tuned against published clinical-AI deployment data.

item.title: v0.0.7 — Evidence bundles ship.
item.description: USTAR tar emit + offline-verify. Bundle's manifest signed by the same key as the ledger.

item.title: v0.0.5 — Drift on the ground floor.
item.description: DriftMonitor with PSI / KS / EMD on per-feature reference vs. current histograms. Severity bands match industry conventional thresholds.

item.title: v0.0.3 — Policy chain composed.
item.description: First version of the PolicyChain with allow/modify/block verdicts. PHI scrubber + JSON-Schema-subset validator.

item.title: v0.0.1 — Genesis. The Merkle ledger compiles.
item.description: First commit. Append-only signed log over SQLite. verify() walks the chain. No policies, no consent, no drift — just the substrate's spine.
